channel.title: cascivo blog
channel.description: Notes on building cascivo: the AI layer, signal-driven reactivity, modern CSS, and what shipping an owned-code design system actually looks like.

item.title: Giving your AI coding agent a component library it can’t hallucinate
item.description: AI agents write plausible-looking UI code that invents props, colors, and class names that don’t exist. cascivo ships a machine-readable manifest per component so agents can select, scaffold, and validate their own output instead of guessing.

item.title: Signals vs. useState: what actually changes
item.description: cascivo components use signals instead of useState — not as a style preference, but because it changes what re-renders. Here’s what that means in practice, and where the line for "genuine internal state" actually sits.

item.title: Owned-code components: what shadcn got right, and where we took it further
item.description: shadcn/ui’s biggest idea wasn’t a component set — it was making you own the code instead of depending on it. cascivo keeps that idea and builds the rest of what you’d otherwise have to hand-roll on top of it.
